rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute functional collapse with severe orthostatic near-syncope, profound weakness (bedbound) and recurrent hypoglycaemic episodes on a background strongly suggestive of panhypopituitarism (Sheehan syndrome) — i.e. an acute secondary adrenal crisis with impending cardiovascular collapse. This is an immediate threat to life: T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier. The patient herself reports time-critical features (near-syncope/collapse on standing, episodes of confusion), so the emergency is patient-reportable and not merely an exam finding — the T3 telehealth-uplift ceiling does not apply. Definitive treatment (IV hydrocortisone + fluids) is a hospital action reached only via 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Ongoing near-syncope/collapse on any attempt to sit up or stand; profound weakness / bedbound
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Now — call 000 immediately and keep her lying flat
escalation_edges.rationale: Severe orthostatic decompensation signals adrenal crisis with collapse risk; emergency ambulance and hospital resuscitation are required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This is an emergency — call 000 for an ambulance now and tell them she keeps nearly passing out and can't stay upright. Keep her lying flat until they arrive.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of consciousness, seizure, or becoming very drowsy/unrousable (hypoglycaemia / shock)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately if it occurs before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.rationale: Hypoglycaemia and shock can cause seizures and coma; airway protection and paramedic resuscitation are critical.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she passes out or has a seizure, roll her onto her side into the recovery position, keep her warm, and tell the 000 dispatcher straight away.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Bedbound patient with an adult child present as the sole carer and informant
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct the carer to keep her lying flat and warm, stay with her, unlock/open the door for paramedics, and gather old obstetric records — while 000 is called.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Adult with a slow two-year background of deterioration now acutely decompensated
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do not be reassured by the chronic history — the acute change is the emergency; escalate on it.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say she keeps nearly fainting, can't stay upright, and is severely weak with episodes of confusion. Keep her lying flat — do not let her sit up or stand. Keep her warm. Stay with her, unlock the front door for the paramedics, and have any old records about the difficult childbirth ready. If she passes out or has a seizure, roll her onto her side and tell 000 immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a routine appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary and specialist endocrine plan; ensure the GP and endocrinologist receive it, along with the obstetric history of severe postpartum haemorrhage.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as chronic fatigue, an eating disorder, isolated hypothyroidism or a routine endocrine referral, and not escalating to emergency care.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Untreated adrenal crisis progresses to hypotensive/hypoglycaemic collapse, shock and death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising urgency but advising self-transport to ED rather than an emergency ambulance — risking collapse or hypoglycaemic seizure en route without paramedic support in a bedbound, hypotensive patient.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact endocrine diagnosis (panhypopituitarism / secondary adrenal crisis) cannot be confirmed via telehealth — it needs hospital bloods, a hormone panel and imaging.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the recognised emergency pattern: escalate to 000 immediately regardless of diagnostic uncertainty, and give patient-doable holding advice (stay supine, keep warm).
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Waiting to confirm the diagnosis, arranging outpatient tests, or reassuring the patient because the cause is not yet proven.
